notable.notable_blurb: Union artillery general who commanded the Reserve at Gettysburg

military_service.unit: Artillery Reserve, Army of the Potomac
military_service.notes: Commanded the Artillery Reserve at Gettysburg; severely wounded at Cold Harbor; deputy quartermaster general after the war.

landmarks.landmark_blurb: Commanded the Army of the Potomac's Artillery Reserve, honored by this Gettysburg monument.

cross_connections.display_label: was his uncle and also a Union brigadier general

narrative_blocks.header: His guns helped break Pickett's Charge
narrative_blocks.body: At Gettysburg he commanded the Army of the Potomac's Artillery Reserve, whose massed guns helped repel the Confederate assault on the third day. Two horses were shot from under him during the fighting.

narrative_blocks.header: Nephew of the general who founded Anniston
narrative_blocks.body: His uncle Daniel Tyler also wore a Union general's star and later founded the Alabama city of Anniston. Both men descended from the theologian Jonathan Edwards through their Connecticut family.

narrative_blocks.header: Shot at Cold Harbor, he never rode again
narrative_blocks.body: A bullet shattered his ankle at Cold Harbor in 1864, ending his field command. He was brevetted major general for gallantry and served out the war at a desk.

narrative_blocks.header: He finished his memoir, then died at forty-two
narrative_blocks.body: His war injuries wore at his health, and he died young. He had just completed an autobiography, published in 1878 and still in print.

narrative_blocks.header: He soldiered the frontier before the war
narrative_blocks.body: Before the Civil War he campaigned in the Yakima War in the Pacific Northwest and the Utah War, crossing the young frontier as an artillery officer. Gettysburg lay years ahead.

burialCemetery.name: Cedar Hill Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Major Hartford garden cemetery. Contains graves of J.P. Morgan Sr., Mark Twain, and many prominent Hartford families.

INST035.name: United States Military Academy (West Point)
INST035.notes: Founded 1802 at West Point NY. HOOKER TREE CONNECTION: Rev. George Washington Woodbridge (HD2163), son of General Jonathan Woodbridge (H00928), graduated West Point; served as 2nd Lieutenant US Army 1826-1829 before entering the Episcopal ministry. He became rector of Monumental Church Richmond VA 1843-1878 and escorted Flora Cooke Stuart to her dying husband Jeb Stuart in May 1864.
